message:
feat(layout): add dependency graph, incremental engine, and enhanced pane physics

Introduce two major new layout subsystems and significantly expand pane
interaction physics:

Dependency graph (dep_graph.rs, 1034 lines):
- DAG-based dependency tracking for incremental layout invalidation
- Supports dirty-flag propagation, hash-based dedup, and topological
  traversal for efficient re-layout of only affected subtrees

Incremental layout engine (incremental.rs, 1153 lines):
- Builds on dep_graph to provide incremental layout recomputation
- Avoids full-tree relayout when only a subset of panes change,
  critical for interactive resize/drag performance

Pane physics enhancements (pane.rs, +324 lines):
- Rework PaneInertialThrow::from_motion() with proper direction
  normalization, speed curves, and noise penalties
- Improve PanePressureSnapProfile::from_motion() with axis-dominance
  weighting for more natural snap behavior
- Add elastic_ratio_bps() for edge-band resistance during resize
- Add dock_zone_rank(), dock_zone_motion_intent(), and motion-aware
  dock preview collection for directional alignment scoring
- Integrate elastic_ratio_bps into all split-ratio resize paths
- Update plan_reflow_move_with_preview to use motion-aware docking
  with magnetic boost
- Add 4 unit tests for pressure, dock intent, and inertial throw

Module exposure and benchmarks:
- Expose dep_graph and incremental modules in ftui-layout lib.rs
- Add criterion benchmark group for 10K-node dependency graph
  operations (dirty checks, mark/propagate, hash-dedup, clean_all)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
